goal: Functional xiaozhi-like voice assistant experience with backend deployed on AX650 and frontend running on PC, focused first on convenient deployment.
stack: Python FastAPI backend, static HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend, shell deployment scripts

requirement_alignment.goal: Recreate the xiaozhi-like functional experience, not protocol compatibility, with AX650 running the backend and PC browser running the frontend.
non_goals: Do not implement xiaozhi-esp32 protocol compatibility in the first iteration.
Do not add MQTT+UDP, ESP32 firmware support, or MCP device control in the first iteration.
Do not add new model inference capabilities in the first iteration.
current_state_summary: The repository already has FastAPI REST and WebSocket dialogue APIs.
The backend already has ASR/LLM/TTS/Speaker provider scaffolding and a static web console.
The backend has model download support via huggingface_hub with hf-mirror defaults.
The main gap is a repeatable AX650 backend plus PC frontend deployment path.
acceptance_checks: AX650 has one command path to install backend dependencies, optionally download selected models, generate env defaults, and start the backend.
PC has one command path to launch the static frontend pointed at an AX650 backend URL.
The deployment scripts are non-destructive, configurable, and include health checks.
Documentation presents the AX650 backend plus PC frontend path as the primary deployment flow.
requirement_alignment.validation_approach: Run backend pytest suite and inspect script help/output for non-destructive deployment behavior.
requirement_alignment.first_iteration_scope: Add deploy scripts and documentation for AX650 backend setup/run and PC frontend launch; wire them to existing model download and env conventions.

issues.title: feat[1]: add AX650 backend and PC frontend deployment flow
acceptance_checks: AX650 backend setup script installs base dependencies and optional model download dependencies without destructive actions.
AX650 backend run script prepares env defaults, starts uvicorn, and performs a health check.
PC frontend script serves frontend/static and points users to the AX650 backend URL.
Documentation presents this as the primary deployment path and explains model download/provider enabling boundaries.
cd backend && python3 -m pytest tests passes.

rounds.summary: Added AX650 backend setup/run scripts, PC static frontend launch flow, model download helper, static web console, and deployment documentation.

current_iteration.implementation_summary: Added AX650 setup/run scripts, PC frontend launch script, URL-based frontend backend configuration, model download requirements, and deployment documentation.

assumptions: AX650 board has Python 3 available and can install Python packages from configured indexes.
Model download uses huggingface_hub and defaults to https://hf-mirror.com.
Frontend can be served by Python's built-in http.server on the PC.
Real AXEngine provider validation will remain provider-specific and outside this first iteration unless scripts can call existing checks safely.
